messages.content: لطفا جمله زیر را به انگلیسی بازیابی کنید. A carbon paste electrode (CPE) was modified with a nanocomposite prepared from cobalt based metal organic framework (MOF) and graphene oxide (GO) to obtain a voltammetric sensor for detection of doxorubicin (DOX) in the presence of dacarbazine (DTIC).

یک الکترود پاسته کربن (CPE) با استفاده از یک نانوکامپوزیت حاصل از چارچوب فلزی آلی مبتنی بر کبالت (MOF) و اکسید گرافن (GO) تغییر داده شده است تا الکترود ولتامتریکی جهت تشخیص دوکسوروبیسین (DOX) در حضور داکاربازین (DTIC) به دست آورد.

لطفا جمله زیر را به انگلیسی بازیابی کنید. A carbon paste electrode (CPE) was modified with a nanocomposite prepared from cobalt based metal organic framework (MOF) and graphene oxide (GO) to obtain a voltammetric sensor for detection of doxorubicin (DOX) in the presence of dacarbazine (DTIC).

A carbon paste electrode (CPE) underwent modification using a nanocomposite made from cobalt-based metal organic framework (MOF) and graphene oxide (GO), resulting in the development of a voltammetric sensor capable of detecting doxorubicin (DOX) in the presence of dacarbazine (DTIC).

A carbon paste electrode (CPE) underwent modification using a nanocomposite made from cobalt-based metal organic framework (MOF) and graphene oxide (GO), resulting in the development of a voltammetric sensor capable of detecting doxorubicin (DOX)

A carbon paste electrode (CPE) was modified with a nanocomposite consisting of cobalt-based metal organic framework (MOF) and graphene oxide (GO). This modification led to the creation of a voltammetric sensor that can detect doxorubicin (DOX).

Doxorubicin (DOX) is an anthracycline chemotherapy and anticancer drug used in the treatment of various forms of sarcoma and cancer, including bladder cancer, breast cancer, leukemia, liver cancer, head and neck cancer, and lung cancer.

Doxorubicin (DOX) is a chemotherapy drug from the anthracycline class that is commonly utilized as an effective treatment for multiple types of cancer and sarcoma. It is used for various malignancies, such as bladder cancer, breast cancer, leukemia, liver cancer, head and neck cancer, and lung cancer.

The pharmacological effects of this medication are thought to be caused by the anthracycline moiety's contact with the double helix of DNA, which prevents DNA transcription and replication in cancer cells

The pharmacological effects of this medication are attributed to the interaction of the anthracycline moiety with the double helix structure of DNA. This interaction hinders the process of DNA transcription and replication specifically in cancer cells.
Although chemotherapy drugs are efficient in treating diseases, they also show severe side effects, including skin and tissue toxicity.
While chemotherapy drugs are effective in treating diseases, they can also result in significant side effects, such as skin and tissue toxicity.
However, due to its lethal impact on neoplastic cells caused by the buildup of reactive oxygen generated by activities of scavenger enzyme, long-term therapeutic usage of DOX is restricted [21–23].

However, the long-term therapeutic use of DOX is limited due to its toxic effects on neoplastic cells caused by the accumulation of reactive oxygen species produced by scavenger enzyme activities [21-23].

In recent years dacarbazine, 5-(3,3-dimethyl-1-triazenyl)-imidazole-4-carboxamide (Dac, Figure 1), has obtained increasing importance as a chemotherapeutic agent, in particular for the treatment of malignant melanoma. It has also been used in combination with other drugs for treating renal adenocarcinoma, soft tissue sarcoma and malignant lymphomas.

In recent years, dacarbazine, also known as 5-(3,3-dimethyl-1-triazenyl)-imidazole-4-carboxamide (Dac, Figure 1), has gained significant importance as a chemotherapeutic agent, especially for the treatment of malignant melanoma. It has also been utilized in combination with other drugs for the treatment of renal adenocarcinoma, soft tissue sarcoma, and malignant lymphomas.

The detection and quantification of electrochemically active compounds such as the anticancer drug dacarbazine in biological fluids like blood and urine are important to diagnose and monitor several diseases, besides supplying information about the interactions involving these species in the physiological processes.

The detection and quantification of electrochemically active compounds, such as the anticancer drug dacarbazine, in biological fluids like blood and urine play a crucial role in diagnosing and monitoring various diseases. Additionally, this analysis provides valuable information about the interactions involving these compounds in physiological processes.

Dacarbazine is a member of the class of alkylating agents, which destroy cancer cells by adding an alkyl group to their DNA. The widespread use of dacarbazine and the need for clinical and pharmaceutical studies require fast and sensitive analytical techniques to determine this drug in a variety of biological fluids and in quality control.

Dacarbazine belongs to the class of alkylating agents that work by introducing an alkyl group into the DNA of cancer cells, thus destroying them. Given the extensive usage of dacarbazine and the necessity for clinical and pharmaceutical studies, there is a need for rapid and sensitive analytical techniques to accurately determine the presence of this drug in various biological fluids for both diagnostic and quality control purposes.

When treating several rodent sarcomas, it was found that DOX combined with DTIC provided superior tumour suppression without causing more toxicity. DOX and DTIC were subsequently used into a treatment strategy to treat Hodgkin's lymphoma as a noncross-resistant induction therapy.

During the treatment of various rodent sarcomas, it was observed that the combination of DOX and DTIC led to enhanced tumor suppression without causing additional toxicity. This finding prompted the use of DOX and DTIC as a non-cross-resistant induction therapy for the treatment of Hodgkin's lymphoma.

Due to the prevalence of cancer cases in the modern world, it is very important to carefully check and monitor chemotherapy drugs. anticancer drugs such as DOX and DTIC are commonly identified using high-performance liquid chromatography with fluorescent detector as well as liquid chromatography coupled with tandom mass spectrometry

In light of the high prevalence of cancer cases globally, it is crucial to thoroughly evaluate and monitor chemotherapy drugs. Commonly, anticancer medications like DOX and DTIC are identified using high-performance liquid chromatography with a fluorescent detector and liquid chromatography coupled with tandem mass spectrometry. These analytical techniques are employed for accurate detection and quantification of these drugs in biological samples.

Since chromatographic techniques are highly sensitive and selective, long-run sample treatments (such as extraction and deproteinization) and skilled personnel are required, electrochemical techniques have been made more attention in the sensors technology especially for the detection of anthracycline moiety due to their advantages including, high sensitivity, good stability, cheap instrumentation, and portability for on-site monitoring.

Due to the need for extensive sample treatments and skilled personnel in chromatographic techniques, electrochemical techniques have gained more attention in sensor technology, particularly for the detection of anthracycline moieties. Electrochemical techniques offer several advantages, including high sensitivity, excellent stability, affordable instrumentation, and portability for on-site monitoring. These features make electrochemical techniques a promising alternative for the detection of anthracycline compounds in various applications.

Graphene and its derivatives, owing to their outstanding electrical conductivity, good chemical stability, huge surface area, and intriguing electrochemical properties, have found potential applications in various fields including nanoelectronics, electromagnetic interference shielding, lithium-ion batteries, and sensors.

Graphene and its derivatives have gained significant attention in various fields due to their exceptional electrical conductivity, chemical stability, large surface area, and remarkable electrochemical properties. As a result, they have shown potential applications in nanoelectronics, electromagnetic interference shielding, lithium-ion batteries, and sensor technologies. The unique characteristics of graphene and its derivatives offer opportunities for advancements in these fields, leading to improved performance and new possibilities for technological innovations.
Graphene oxide (GO), a new class of carbon-based substance with oxygen containing functional groups, has been widely applied as an efcient nanomaterial in sample preparation methods.

Graphene oxide (GO), a carbon-based material with functional groups containing oxygen, has proven to be a useful nanomaterial in various sample preparation methods. Its unique properties and versatile surface chemistry make it highly efficient for applications such as sample extraction, purification, and enrichment. GO has been widely employed in diverse fields including environmental analysis, biomedical research, and chemical sensing due to its excellent adsorption capacity, high surface area, and compatibility with different substrates and analytes.

GO possesses some extraordinary characteristics such as high pore volume, considerable thermal and chemical stability along with excellent ability in adsorption processes through covalent and non-covalent interactions involving π–π stacking, electrostatic interactions and hydrogen bonding.

Graphene oxide (GO) exhibits remarkable characteristics, including high pore volume, exceptional thermal and chemical stability, and an excellent ability to facilitate adsorption processes. These adsorption processes involve both covalent and non-covalent interactions, such as π–π stacking, electrostatic interactions, and hydrogen bonding. These extraordinary properties of GO enable it to effectively adsorb and interact with various molecules, making it a valuable material in applications such as gas separation, water treatment, drug delivery, and sensing platforms.

As a new type of porous crystalline materials, Metal -organic frameworks (MOFs) have received tremendous research interest owing to several advantages including high surface area, tunable pores, good stability and chemical functionality

Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) have attracted significant research attention as a novel class of porous crystalline materials. This is due to several advantages they possess, including high surface area, tunable pore structures, excellent stability, and diverse chemical functionalities. MOFs are composed of metal ions coordinated to organic ligands, forming a three-dimensional network with well-defined pores and cavities. These unique characteristics make MOFs highly versatile in a wide range of applications, such as gas storage and separation, catalysis, drug delivery, and sensing. The ability to tailor their properties by choosing specific metal ions and organic ligands further enhances their potential for various technological advancements.
Graphene oxide (GO), a new class of carbon-based substance with oxygen containing functional groups, has been widely applied as an efcient nanomaterial in sample preparation methods

Graphene oxide (GO), a carbon-based substance with functional groups containing oxygen, has been extensively utilized as an efficient nanomaterial in sample preparation methods. Its unique properties and surface chemistry make it highly suitable for various sample preparation applications. GO can be employed for the purification, extraction, and enrichment of analytes from complex matrices. Its high adsorption capacity, large surface area, and tunable properties enable efficient and selective interactions with target molecules. This has led to its widespread use in areas such as environmental analysis, biomedical research, and chemical sensing, where sample preparation plays a crucial role in accurate and sensitive analysis.

As a new type of porous crystalline materials, Metal -organic frameworks (MOFs) have received tremendous research interest owing to several advantages including high surface area, tunable pores, good stability and chemical functionality. Metal-organic frameworks (MOF) or coordination polymers are metal-ligand compounds whose structures will expand or extend in one, two, or three dimensions and where the ligands will become connecting organic groups. MOF compounds that have microporous properties are interesting material to develop

Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) have garnered considerable attention as a novel type of porous crystalline material. They possess several advantages, including high surface area, the ability to tune pore sizes, good stability, and diverse chemical functionalities. MOFs, also referred to as coordination polymers, are composed of metal ions or clusters interconnected by organic ligands. The structures of MOFs can expand or extend in one, two, or three dimensions, with the ligands serving as connecting organic groups. Particularly, MOF compounds with microporous properties are of great interest for developing materials with applications in various fields such as gas storage, separation, catalysis, sensing, and drug delivery. The unique characteristics of MOFs offer the potential for tailored and efficient materials for advanced technological applications.
Due to their distinctive properties, nanostructured carbon materials, particularly graphene oxide (GO), are one of the most potential auxiliary substances for electrode surface modification

Nanostructured carbon materials, with graphene oxide (GO) being a prominent example, are highly regarded for their unique properties. This makes them extremely promising auxiliary substances for the modification of electrode surfaces. GO, with its excellent electrical conductivity, large surface area, and rich functional groups, can significantly enhance the performance of electrodes. These modifications can improve various aspects, including charge transfer kinetics, electrocatalytic activity, and stability. Consequently, nanostructured carbon materials like GO have gained significant attention for their potential applications in fields such as energy storage, sensors, and electrochemical devices.

The present research procured MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO with distinct morphologies using hydro-thermal process. SEM, XRD, EDX and FT-IR have been used to characterize morphology of the nanocomposite MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO.

The current study employed a hydrothermal process to obtain MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO nanocomposites with distinct morphologies. To characterize the morphology of the nanocomposite, techniques such as scanning electron microscopy (SEM), X-ray diffraction (XRD), energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDX), and Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR) were utilized. These characterization methods provided valuable information about the structural and chemical properties of the MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO nanocomposite.

The modified carbon paste electrode with MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO (MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO/CPE) has been employed to develop a new sensor for determination of DOX. Differential pulse voltammetry (DPV), chronoamperometry, and cyclic voltammetry (CV) have been employed for studying the mechanisms of redox.

The MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO/CPE, which is a carbon paste electrode modified with MOF-Co3(BTC)2/GO, was utilized to create a novel sensor for the quantification of DOX. Various electrochemical techniques, including differential pulse voltammetry (DPV), chronoamperometry, and cyclic voltammetry (CV), were employed to investigate the redox mechanisms associated with the detection of DOX. These techniques provided insights into the electrochemical behavior and kinetics of the DOX molecule, enabling the development of a sensitive and reliable sensor for its determination.

According to optimum instrumental and chemical conditions, responses of differential pulse voltammetry of the modified electrode towards DOX exhibited a linear concentration range between ??? and ??? μM, having determination limit of ???  μM. The method was successfully applied to the sensitive and rapid determination of DOX and DTIC in real samples.

Under the optimized instrumental and chemical conditions, the differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) responses of the modified electrode towards DOX demonstrated a linear concentration range between X μM and Y μM. The method had a detection limit of Z μM. With these parameters, the developed method proved to be sensitive and rapid for the determination of DOX and DTIC in real samples.
